prompt: Production issue: Slow query performance in RDS PostgreSQL
response: Slow queries impact application performance. Diagnostic approach: 1) Enable pg_stat_statements extension, 2) Identify slow queries: SELECT query, calls, mean_time, total_time FROM pg_stat_statements ORDER BY total_time DESC LIMIT 10, 3) Analyze query plan: EXPLAIN ANALYZE SELECT..., 4) Look for sequential scans on large tables, 5) Missing indexes. Resolution: Add indexes on columns used in WHERE, JOIN, ORDER BY: CREATE INDEX idx_orders_customer ON orders(customer_id), update table statistics: ANALYZE table_name, rewrite queries to be more efficient (avoid SELECT *, use appropriate JOINs, add WHERE clauses to limit rows). Monitor with CloudWatch: ReadLatency, WriteLatency. Set up Performance Insights for detailed analysis. For complex queries, consider materialized views for pre-aggregated data. Review query patterns and add composite indexes where beneficial.

prompt: Authorization missing for company code 1000
response: SAP authorization error indicates user lacks permission. Resolution: 1) Check missing authorizations using SU53 (must run immediately after error), 2) Identify authorization object causing issue (typically F_BKPF_BUK for company code), 3) Add company code to user's authorization profile, 4) Assign appropriate role using PFCG, 5) Test authorization after changes. In PFCG: display role, go to Authorizations tab, edit authorization object F_BKPF_BUK, add company code 1000 to allowed values, generate profile, assign role to user. For technical user: ensure integration user has authorizations for all company codes used in interface. Implement authorization check before posting: AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK' ID 'BUKRS' FIELD '1000'. Document required authorizations in interface specification.

prompt: Urgent: RDS database connection timeout
response: Connection timeout indicates network or security group issues. Troubleshooting steps: 1) Verify security group allows inbound traffic on port 5432 (PostgreSQL) or 3306 (MySQL) from application security group, 2) Check connection string format and credentials, 3) Test connectivity from application server using telnet or psql/mysql client, 4) Verify NAT Gateway exists if database is in private subnet, 5) Check route tables for proper routing. Common fix: Add inbound rule to RDS security group allowing Custom TCP on port 5432 from source security group of application servers. Also verify the endpoint URL is correct and database instance is running.

prompt: How to add interface mapping for Cost Center AMEX123?
response: This is a data setup problem in the Interface Mapping widget. To add Cost Center mapping: 1) Login to Web UI, 2) Navigate to Interface Mapping widget, 3) Select 'Cost Center' mapping type, 4) Click 'Add New Mapping', 5) Enter CTRM Cost Center: ENERGY999, 6) Enter SAP Cost Center: 4711ENERGY, 7) Select Business Unit: TRADE001, 8) Click 'Save'. Verify the mapping by testing with a sample trade. If the SAP cost center doesn't exist, create it first using transaction KS01.

prompt: Lambda out of memory error
response: Out of memory error means Lambda exceeded allocated memory. Default is 128MB. Resolution: 1) Increase memory allocation in Lambda configuration (also increases CPU proportionally), 2) Optimize code to use less memory, 3) Stream large files instead of loading entirely, 4) Implement pagination for large datasets. To increase: AWS Console > Lambda > Configuration > General > Memory, increase to 512MB or higher based on needs. Monitor MemorySize and MemoryUsed metrics. Best practices: use generators for large data processing, close database connections after use, clear large variables when done, avoid global variables holding large data. For very large processing (>10GB), consider using ECS Fargate or EC2 instead of Lambda. Enable detailed monitoring to track memory usage patterns.

prompt: SQS message visibility timeout causing duplicate processing
response: Duplicate processing occurs when visibility timeout is too short. When Lambda doesn't complete processing before timeout expires, message becomes visible again and is processed by another instance. Resolution: 1) Increase SQS visibility timeout to match or exceed Lambda timeout + buffer, 2) If Lambda takes 5 minutes, set visibility timeout to 6+ minutes, 3) Enable dead letter queue (DLQ) for messages that fail repeatedly, 4) Implement idempotency keys in application to handle duplicates gracefully. Configuration: AWS Console > SQS > Queue > Edit > Visibility timeout. Formula: Visibility timeout = Lambda timeout Ã— 6. Also implement unique message ID tracking in application to detect and skip duplicates. Monitor using CloudWatch metric ApproximateAgeOfOldestMessage.

prompt: High database CPU utilization in RDS
response: High CPU (>75%) indicates performance bottleneck. Causes: 1) Inefficient queries, 2) Missing indexes, 3) Too many connections, 4) Complex joins without optimization. Resolution: Enable Performance Insights in RDS console, identify expensive queries: SELECT query, calls, total_time FROM pg_stat_statements ORDER BY total_time DESC LIMIT 10, analyze query plans: EXPLAIN ANALYZE SELECT..., add appropriate indexes, optimize or rewrite slow queries, consider read replicas to offload read traffic, upgrade instance class if needed. For immediate relief: kill long-running queries: SELECT pg_terminate_backend(pid) FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE state = 'active' AND query_start < now() - interval '10 minutes'. Review CloudWatch metrics CPUUtilization, DatabaseConnections, ReadLatency.

prompt: Need help: API Gateway CORS error in browser
response: CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) error occurs when browser blocks request from different origin. Symptoms: works in Postman but fails in browser, see CORS error in browser console. Resolution: 1) Enable CORS in API Gateway for the API resource, 2) Add CORS headers to Lambda response, 3) Handle OPTIONS preflight requests. Required headers: Access-Control-Allow-Origin (set to specific domain or *), Access-Control-Allow-Headers (Content-Type, Authorization, etc.), Access-Control-Allow-Methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE). In Lambda: return {'statusCode': 200, 'headers': {'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*', 'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': 'Content-Type', 'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'GET,POST,OPTIONS'}, 'body': json.dumps(data)}. For OPTIONS requests, return 200 with CORS headers and empty body. Test from browser developer console.

prompt: ECS task health check failing
response: Health check failures cause ALB to mark targets as unhealthy and stop routing traffic. Common causes: 1) Wrong health check path configured, 2) Health endpoint returns non-200 status, 3) Timeout too short, 4) Application not fully started, 5) Security group blocking ALB access. Resolution: Verify health endpoint URL is correct (e.g., /health not /healthcheck), ensure endpoint returns 200 OK, increase health check timeout and interval if startup is slow, check security group allows ALB to reach task on correct port, review application logs for health check errors. Configure in task definition: healthCheck with command, interval (30s), timeout (5s), retries (3), startPeriod (60s for slow startup). Implement lightweight health check that validates critical dependencies.

Resolution steps: 1) Check memory allocation in ECS task definition, 2) Review CloudWatch Logs for memory usage patterns, 3) Increase memory limit in task definition (memory parameter), 4) Check for memory leaks in application code, 5) Enable container insights for detailed metrics. prompt: In CTRM-SAP integration: SNS message not reaching SQS queue
response: SNS to SQS subscription issue. Messages published but not delivered. Resolution: 1) Check SNS subscription status is 'Confirmed', 2) Verify SQS queue policy allows SNS to send messages, 3) Check SNS topic subscription filter policy, 4) Review dead letter queue for SNS, 5) Verify message format matches subscription filter. SQS policy must allow: {"Effect": "Allow", "Principal": {"Service": "sns.amazonaws.com"}, "Action": "sqs:SendMessage", "Resource": "queue-arn", "Condition": {"ArnEquals": {"aws:SourceArn": "topic-arn"}}}. Check subscription filter if used - messages not matching filter are dropped. Monitor SNS metrics: NumberOfNotificationsFailed. Test with direct SQS send first to isolate SNS. Check SNS subscription endpoint is correct SQS queue ARN. Enable CloudWatch Logs for SNS delivery status.

prompt: Production issue: Lambda function timeout after 30 seconds
response: Lambda timeout indicates the function didn't complete within configured limit. Resolution: 1) Increase Lambda timeout in configuration (maximum 15 minutes), 2) Optimize code to run faster, 3) For longer operations, implement async pattern returning 202 Accepted immediately and processing in background, 4) Check if external dependencies (database, API) are slow. To increase timeout: AWS Console > Lambda > Configuration > General > Timeout, or CLI: aws lambda update-function-configuration --function-name my-func --timeout 300. For operations that legitimately take longer than 15 minutes, use Step Functions or push to SQS queue for async processing. Monitor CloudWatch metrics to identify bottlenecks.

In FBN1: select company code 1000, select fiscal year 2024, select number range object RF_BELEG, click 'Change' button, increase 'To Number' in interval (e.g., from 999999999 to 9999999999), save. Immediate workaround: use different document type with available number range if business process allows. Prevention: Monitor number range usage monthly, set up alerts at 80% utilization, implement year-based number ranges, document number range strategy. For high-volume interfaces, use larger initial intervals.

prompt: Document balance error: debit 1000.00 credit 900.00
response: SAP document must balance (debit = credit). This error indicates a 100.00 difference. Causes: 1) Incorrect line item amounts in source data, 2) Rounding errors in calculation, 3) Missing offsetting entry, 4) Currency conversion issues. Resolution: Verify all line items in CTRM source, check transformation logic for rounding errors (use proper decimal precision), ensure offsetting account is included, review currency conversion rates and rounding. For rounding: use decimal.Decimal in Python or round() with appropriate precision. Add balancing line item if needed to correct small rounding differences. Check BAPI return messages for specific details. Implement validation before posting: sum(debits) must equal sum(credits).

prompt: Trade data sync failing with JSON parse error
response: JSON parse error indicates malformed data from CTRM export. Causes: 1) Invalid JSON syntax, 2) Unescaped special characters, 3) Truncated data, 4) Wrong encoding. Resolution: Validate JSON using online validator or: python -m json.tool file.json, check for unescaped quotes or backslashes, verify file is complete (not truncated), ensure UTF-8 encoding, review CTRM export configuration. Common fixes: escape special characters in string fields, wrap all string values in quotes, use proper array and object syntax, validate JSON schema before processing. Implement try-catch in Lambda: try: data = json.loads(body) except json.JSONDecodeError as e: log error with line number and character position. Add validation layer before processing to catch issues early.

prompt: In CTRM-SAP integration: Message transformation error: field type mismatch
response: Type mismatch occurs when source data type doesn't match SAP field requirements. Common examples: string sent for numeric field, numeric sent for character field, invalid date format. Resolution: 1) Identify field and expected type from error message, 2) Check source data format, 3) Implement type conversion in transformation layer, 4) Add validation before transformation. Type conversion examples: string to number: float(value) or int(value), number to string: str(value), date conversion: datetime.strptime(date_str, '%Y-%m-%d').strftime('%Y%m%d'). Add try-catch for conversion errors: try: amount = float(value) except ValueError: raise ValidationError(f'Invalid amount: {value}'). Implement schema validation using JSON schema or pydantic models. Document type requirements for each SAP field in interface specification.

prompt: Cannot pull container image from ECR
response: CannotPullContainerError indicates image pull failure. Common causes: 1) No internet access from private subnet (NAT Gateway missing), 2) Insufficient ECR permissions on task execution role, 3) Image doesn't exist or wrong tag, 4) VPC endpoint misconfiguration. Resolution: Verify NAT Gateway exists in public subnet with route in private subnet route table, ensure task execution role has ECR permissions (ecr:GetAuthorizationToken, ecr:BatchCheckLayerAvailability, ecr:GetDownloadUrlForLayer, ecr:BatchGetImage), confirm image exists: aws ecr describe-images --repository-name my-repo, check security group allows outbound HTTPS (port 443). Alternative: use VPC endpoints for ECR (com.amazonaws.region.ecr.api and com.amazonaws.region.ecr.dkr) to avoid NAT Gateway costs.

prompt: DLQ has messages but not processing
response: Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) collects messages that failed processing repeatedly. These require manual intervention. Resolution: 1) Retrieve messages from DLQ for analysis, 2) Identify failure pattern (data issue, code bug, infrastructure problem), 3) Fix root cause, 4) Reprocess valid messages, 5) Purge invalid messages. To retrieve: aws sqs receive-message --queue-url https://sqs.region.amazonaws.com/account/my-dlq. Common causes: poison pill messages with invalid data, code bugs that cause exception, timeout issues, external dependency failures. Process: fix the issue in code, redrive messages back to main queue: aws sqs start-message-move-task --source-arn <dlq-arn> --destination-arn <main-queue-arn>, or process manually with Lambda. Implement DLQ alarms: alert when NumberOfMessagesSent to DLQ > 0. Document DLQ handling procedures.

prompt: Production issue: High cache miss rate in ElastiCache
response: High cache miss rate (below 80%) indicates ineffective caching. Causes: 1) Cache too small (frequent evictions), 2) TTL too short, 3) Poor key distribution, 4) Cache not warmed up. Resolution: Check eviction metrics in CloudWatch, increase cache node size if memory pressure is high, adjust TTL values based on data volatility (longer for stable data), implement cache warming for frequently accessed data, optimize key design for better distribution. For monitoring: aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics --metric-name CacheHitRate. Target is >80% hit rate. Consider using allkeys-lru eviction policy for automatic management. Diagnostic steps: 1) Enable Performance Insights in CloudWatch, 2) Check BAPI execution time in SAP using ST12 transaction, 3) Review network latency between middleware and SAP, 4) Verify database performance in SAP (ST04), 5) Check for table locks or long-running transactions. Optimization: Use BAPI batch operations instead of individual calls, implement connection pooling, add appropriate database indexes in SAP, check SAP system resources (CPU, memory), review BAPI authorization checks (can be slow). Monitor using CloudWatch metrics. If BAPI consistently slow, consider async processing pattern or request SAP Basis team to tune BAPI.

prompt: Need help: Payment terms Z030 is not defined in SAP
response: Payment terms error indicates configuration missing in SAP. Payment terms control invoice due dates and cash discounts. Resolution: 1) Check payment terms configuration using OBB8, 2) Create payment terms if missing, 3) Update interface mapping with valid payment terms, 4) Verify payment terms in vendor master matches. In OBB8: click 'New Entries', enter payment terms Z030, enter description (e.g., 'Net 30 days'), define payment terms details (baseline date, payment period), save. Common payment terms: Z000 (immediate), Z030 (net 30 days), Z230 (2% discount 10 days, net 30 days). Update vendor master (XK02) with correct payment terms. In interface mapping, map CTRM payment terms to SAP payment terms codes. Standardize payment terms codes across systems to avoid mapping complexity.

prompt: Production issue: Error: Server responded with HTTP 403 Forbidden
response: HTTP 403 means the server refused to authorize your request. Common causes in CTRM-SAP: 1) Missing SAP authorization - check SU53 for details, 2) IP not whitelisted - verify network access, 3) Insufficient permissions - user may lack required role, 4) Resource access denied - verify you own the resource. Resolution: Check user authorizations in SAP using SU53, review role assignments with PFCG, confirm business unit access rights, verify authorization objects F_BKPF_BUK for company code and F_BKPF_GSB for business area.

prompt: Urgent: ECS Fargate container won't start - exit code 137
response: Exit code 137 means the container was killed due to out-of-memory (OOM). The container exceeded its memory limit. Resolution steps: 1) Check memory allocation in ECS task definition, 2) Review CloudWatch Logs for memory usage patterns, 3) Increase memory limit in task definition (memory parameter), 4) Check for memory leaks in application code, 5) Enable container insights for detailed metrics. To fix: Modify task definition to increase memory from current value (e.g., 512MB to 1024MB), redeploy the service, and monitor memory usage. Consider using memory profiling tools to identify leaks.

prompt: Production issue: Redis cache connection refused on port 6379
response: Connection refused to Redis indicates security group or network issue. Resolution steps: 1) Verify ElastiCache security group allows inbound TCP on port 6379 from application security group, 2) Check Redis cluster is running and available, 3) Test connectivity using redis-cli from application server, 4) Verify VPC configuration and subnets are correct. Common fix: Add security group rule allowing port 6379 from application security group. Test with: redis-cli -h my-cache.amazonaws.com -p 6379 ping (should return PONG). Also check application configuration has correct Redis endpoint and credentials. Enable encryption in-transit if required by security policy.

prompt: Message transformation error: field type mismatch
response: Type mismatch occurs when source data type doesn't match SAP field requirements. Common examples: string sent for numeric field, numeric sent for character field, invalid date format. Resolution: 1) Identify field and expected type from error message, 2) Check source data format, 3) Implement type conversion in transformation layer, 4) Add validation before transformation. Type conversion examples: string to number: float(value) or int(value), number to string: str(value), date conversion: datetime.strptime(date_str, '%Y-%m-%d').strftime('%Y%m%d'). Add try-catch for conversion errors: try: amount = float(value) except ValueError: raise ValidationError(f'Invalid amount: {value}'). Implement schema validation using JSON schema or pydantic models. Document type requirements for each SAP field in interface specification.

prompt: Document balance error: debit 1000.00 credit 900.00
response: SAP document must balance (debit = credit). This error indicates a 100.00 difference. Causes: 1) Incorrect line item amounts in source data, 2) Rounding errors in calculation, 3) Missing offsetting entry, 4) Currency conversion issues. Resolution: Verify all line items in CTRM source, check transformation logic for rounding errors (use proper decimal precision), ensure offsetting account is included, review currency conversion rates and rounding. For rounding: use decimal.Decimal in Python or round() with appropriate precision. Add balancing line item if needed to correct small rounding differences. Check BAPI return messages for specific details. Implement validation before posting: sum(debits) must equal sum(credits).

prompt: Number range exhausted for document type SA
response: Number range exhausted is critical error preventing new postings. Occurs when all numbers in range are used. Resolution: 1) Check current number range using transaction FBN1, 2) Extend number range interval, 3) Create new number range if needed, 4) Plan for larger intervals in future. In FBN1: select company code 1000, select fiscal year 2024, select number range object RF_BELEG, click 'Change' button, increase 'To Number' in interval (e.g., from 999999999 to 9999999999), save. Immediate workaround: use different document type with available number range if business process allows. Prevention: Monitor number range usage monthly, set up alerts at 80% utilization, implement year-based number ranges, document number range strategy. For high-volume interfaces, use larger initial intervals.

prompt: Error: G/L account 400000 does not exist in company code 1000
response: This SAP error means the G/L account is not created for the specified company code. Resolution: 1) Verify G/L account exists using transaction FS00, 2) If missing, create G/L account in company code using FS00, 3) Update interface mapping with correct account number, 4) Check chart of accounts assignment to company code. To create: FS00 > enter account 400000 > enter company code 1000 > Create button > fill in description, account group, control data (currency, tax category) > Save. Preventive measure: Validate all G/L accounts before adding to interface mapping. Use transaction FSP0 to display chart of accounts structure and available account ranges.

prompt: Lambda out of memory error
response: Out of memory error means Lambda exceeded allocated memory. Default is 128MB. Resolution: 1) Increase memory allocation in Lambda configuration (also increases CPU proportionally), 2) Optimize code to use less memory, 3) Stream large files instead of loading entirely, 4) Implement pagination for large datasets. To increase: AWS Console > Lambda > Configuration > General > Memory, increase to 512MB or higher based on needs. Monitor MemorySize and MemoryUsed metrics. Best practices: use generators for large data processing, close database connections after use, clear large variables when done, avoid global variables holding large data. For very large processing (>10GB), consider using ECS Fargate or EC2 instead of Lambda. Enable detailed monitoring to track memory usage patterns.

prompt: Production issue: SAP BAPI error: Posting period 013 2024 is not open
response: This error occurs when trying to post to a closed fiscal period. Resolution: 1) Check period status using transaction OB52, 2) Open the posting period for the relevant company code and fiscal year, 3) Verify user has authorization to open periods, 4) Confirm fiscal year variant configuration. In OB52: Select company code 1000, click on period 013/2024, check both Account type '+' (all) and 'A' (assets), set status to 'Open', save changes. Preventive measure: Implement automated period opening process or calendar-based opening schedule. If period should remain closed, adjust posting date in the interface.

prompt: ECS task health check failing
response: Health check failures cause ALB to mark targets as unhealthy and stop routing traffic. Common causes: 1) Wrong health check path configured, 2) Health endpoint returns non-200 status, 3) Timeout too short, 4) Application not fully started, 5) Security group blocking ALB access. Resolution: Verify health endpoint URL is correct (e.g., /health not /healthcheck), ensure endpoint returns 200 OK, increase health check timeout and interval if startup is slow, check security group allows ALB to reach task on correct port, review application logs for health check errors. Configure in task definition: healthCheck with command, interval (30s), timeout (5s), retries (3), startPeriod (60s for slow startup). Implement lightweight health check that validates critical dependencies.

prompt: In CTRM-SAP integration: ALB returns 502 Bad Gateway error
response: HTTP 502 from ALB means it received an invalid response from the target. Causes: 1) Target returned non-HTTP response, 2) Connection closed prematurely, 3) Target timeout exceeded, 4) Application returned malformed HTTP. Diagnostic steps: Check target health in target group, review ALB access logs in S3 for patterns, verify application logs for errors, ensure application returns valid HTTP response with proper status code and headers. Resolution: If timeout issue, increase target group timeout settings. If application error, fix application to return valid HTTP. If connection issue, check security groups allow ALB to reach targets on correct port. Enable ALB access logs for detailed troubleshooting.

prompt: Authorization missing for company code 1000
response: SAP authorization error indicates user lacks permission. Resolution: 1) Check missing authorizations using SU53 (must run immediately after error), 2) Identify authorization object causing issue (typically F_BKPF_BUK for company code), 3) Add company code to user's authorization profile, 4) Assign appropriate role using PFCG, 5) Test authorization after changes. In PFCG: display role, go to Authorizations tab, edit authorization object F_BKPF_BUK, add company code 1000 to allowed values, generate profile, assign role to user. For technical user: ensure integration user has authorizations for all company codes used in interface. Implement authorization check before posting: AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK' ID 'BUKRS' FIELD '1000'. Document required authorizations in interface specification.

prompt: Cannot pull container image from ECR
response: CannotPullContainerError indicates image pull failure. Common causes: 1) No internet access from private subnet (NAT Gateway missing), 2) Insufficient ECR permissions on task execution role, 3) Image doesn't exist or wrong tag, 4) VPC endpoint misconfiguration. Resolution: Verify NAT Gateway exists in public subnet with route in private subnet route table, ensure task execution role has ECR permissions (ecr:GetAuthorizationToken, ecr:BatchCheckLayerAvailability, ecr:GetDownloadUrlForLayer, ecr:BatchGetImage), confirm image exists: aws ecr describe-images --repository-name my-repo, check security group allows outbound HTTPS (port 443). Alternative: use VPC endpoints for ECR (com.amazonaws.region.ecr.api and com.amazonaws.region.ecr.dkr) to avoid NAT Gateway costs.

prompt: SQS message visibility timeout causing duplicate processing
response: Duplicate processing occurs when visibility timeout is too short. When Lambda doesn't complete processing before timeout expires, message becomes visible again and is processed by another instance. Resolution: 1) Increase SQS visibility timeout to match or exceed Lambda timeout + buffer, 2) If Lambda takes 5 minutes, set visibility timeout to 6+ minutes, 3) Enable dead letter queue (DLQ) for messages that fail repeatedly, 4) Implement idempotency keys in application to handle duplicates gracefully. Configuration: AWS Console > SQS > Queue > Edit > Visibility timeout. Formula: Visibility timeout = Lambda timeout Ã— 6. Also implement unique message ID tracking in application to detect and skip duplicates. Monitor using CloudWatch metric ApproximateAgeOfOldestMessage.

prompt: Need help: API Gateway CORS error in browser
response: CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) error occurs when browser blocks request from different origin. Symptoms: works in Postman but fails in browser, see CORS error in browser console. Resolution: 1) Enable CORS in API Gateway for the API resource, 2) Add CORS headers to Lambda response, 3) Handle OPTIONS preflight requests. Required headers: Access-Control-Allow-Origin (set to specific domain or *), Access-Control-Allow-Headers (Content-Type, Authorization, etc.), Access-Control-Allow-Methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE). In Lambda: return {'statusCode': 200, 'headers': {'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*', 'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': 'Content-Type', 'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'GET,POST,OPTIONS'}, 'body': json.dumps(data)}. For OPTIONS requests, return 200 with CORS headers and empty body. Test from browser developer console.

prompt: Urgent: SNS message not reaching SQS queue
response: SNS to SQS subscription issue. Messages published but not delivered. Resolution: 1) Check SNS subscription status is 'Confirmed', 2) Verify SQS queue policy allows SNS to send messages, 3) Check SNS topic subscription filter policy, 4) Review dead letter queue for SNS, 5) Verify message format matches subscription filter. SQS policy must allow: {"Effect": "Allow", "Principal": {"Service": "sns.amazonaws.com"}, "Action": "sqs:SendMessage", "Resource": "queue-arn", "Condition": {"ArnEquals": {"aws:SourceArn": "topic-arn"}}}. Check subscription filter if used - messages not matching filter are dropped. Monitor SNS metrics: NumberOfNotificationsFailed. Test with direct SQS send first to isolate SNS. Check SNS subscription endpoint is correct SQS queue ARN. Enable CloudWatch Logs for SNS delivery status.

prompt: Cost center POWER888 is not valid on posting date
response: Cost center validity error means the cost center is not active for the posting date. Causes: 1) Cost center expired, 2) Validity dates not configured, 3) Not created yet for the date. Resolution: Check cost center validity in SAP using transaction KS03, extend validity period using KS02 if needed, verify cost center is assigned to correct controlling area and company code, update interface mapping if cost center code changed. In KS02: enter cost center AMEX123, check 'Valid from' and 'Valid to' dates, extend 'Valid to' date to include posting date, save changes. Preventive measure: Regular review of cost center validity periods, implement alerts for expiring cost centers, use standard validity period (e.g., full fiscal year) when creating cost centers.

prompt: High cache miss rate in ElastiCache
response: High cache miss rate (below 80%) indicates ineffective caching. Causes: 1) Cache too small (frequent evictions), 2) TTL too short, 3) Poor key distribution, 4) Cache not warmed up. Resolution: Check eviction metrics in CloudWatch, increase cache node size if memory pressure is high, adjust TTL values based on data volatility (longer for stable data), implement cache warming for frequently accessed data, optimize key design for better distribution. For monitoring: aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics --metric-name CacheHitRate. Target is >80% hit rate. Consider using allkeys-lru eviction policy for automatic management. Implement connection pooling to reduce overhead.

prompt: Urgent: Lambda function timeout after 30 seconds
response: Lambda timeout indicates the function didn't complete within configured limit. Resolution: 1) Increase Lambda timeout in configuration (maximum 15 minutes), 2) Optimize code to run faster, 3) For longer operations, implement async pattern returning 202 Accepted immediately and processing in background, 4) Check if external dependencies (database, API) are slow. To increase timeout: AWS Console > Lambda > Configuration > General > Timeout, or CLI: aws lambda update-function-configuration --function-name my-func --timeout 300. For operations that legitimately take longer than 15 minutes, use Step Functions or push to SQS queue for async processing. Monitor CloudWatch metrics to identify bottlenecks.

prompt: Settlement posting fails: vendor 100234 is blocked

prompt: In CTRM-SAP integration: Slow query performance in RDS PostgreSQL
response: Slow queries impact application performance. Diagnostic approach: 1) Enable pg_stat_statements extension, 2) Identify slow queries: SELECT query, calls, mean_time, total_time FROM pg_stat_statements ORDER BY total_time DESC LIMIT 10, 3) Analyze query plan: EXPLAIN ANALYZE SELECT..., 4) Look for sequential scans on large tables, 5) Missing indexes. Resolution: Add indexes on columns used in WHERE, JOIN, ORDER BY: CREATE INDEX idx_orders_customer ON orders(customer_id), update table statistics: ANALYZE table_name, rewrite queries to be more efficient (avoid SELECT *, use appropriate JOINs, add WHERE clauses to limit rows). Monitor with CloudWatch: ReadLatency, WriteLatency. Set up Performance Insights for detailed analysis. For complex queries, consider materialized views for pre-aggregated data. Review query patterns and add composite indexes where beneficial.

prompt: In CTRM-SAP integration: How to add interface mapping for Cost Center ENERGY572?

To add Cost Center mapping: 1) Login to Web UI, 2) Navigate to Interface Mapping widget, 3) Select 'Cost Center' mapping type, 4) Click 'Add New Mapping', 5) Enter CTRM Cost Center: ENERGY572, 6) Enter SAP Cost Center: 4711ENERGY, 7) Select Business Unit: AMEX001, 8) Click 'Save'. Verify the mapping by testing with a sample trade. If the SAP cost center doesn't exist, create it first using transaction KS01.

prompt: Production issue: API Gateway returning 429 Too Many Requests
response: HTTP 429 indicates rate limit exceeded. Default API Gateway limits: 10,000 requests/second steady state, 5,000 requests burst. Resolution: 1) Check current API usage in CloudWatch, 2) Request limit increase from AWS Support for account-level throttle, 3) Implement client-side retry with exponential backoff, 4) Use API keys and usage plans for better control, 5) Respect Retry-After header in response. For retry logic: wait_time = min(2^attempt, 60) seconds. To create usage plan: aws apigateway create-usage-plan --name BasicPlan --throttle burstLimit=100,rateLimit=50. Also consider caching frequently requested data to reduce backend calls and API invocations.

prompt: DLQ has messages but not processing
response: Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) collects messages that failed processing repeatedly. These require manual intervention. Resolution: 1) Retrieve messages from DLQ for analysis, 2) Identify failure pattern (data issue, code bug, infrastructure problem), 3) Fix root cause, 4) Reprocess valid messages, 5) Purge invalid messages. To retrieve: aws sqs receive-message --queue-url https://sqs.region.amazonaws.com/account/my-dlq. Common causes: poison pill messages with invalid data, code bugs that cause exception, timeout issues, external dependency failures. Process: fix the issue in code, redrive messages back to main queue: aws sqs start-message-move-task --source-arn <dlq-arn> --destination-arn <main-queue-arn>, or process manually with Lambda. Implement DLQ alarms: alert when NumberOfMessagesSent to DLQ > 0. Document DLQ handling procedures.

prompt: High cache miss rate in ElastiCache
response: High cache miss rate (below 80%) indicates ineffective caching. Causes: 1) Cache too small (frequent evictions), 2) TTL too short, 3) Poor key distribution, 4) Cache not warmed up. Resolution: Check eviction metrics in CloudWatch, increase cache node size if memory pressure is high, adjust TTL values based on data volatility (longer for stable data), implement cache warming for frequently accessed data, optimize key design for better distribution. For monitoring: aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics --metric-name CacheHitRate. Target is >80% hit rate. Consider using allkeys-lru eviction policy for automatic management. Implement connection pooling to reduce overhead.

prompt: Lambda out of memory error
response: Out of memory error means Lambda exceeded allocated memory. Default is 128MB. Resolution: 1) Increase memory allocation in Lambda configuration (also increases CPU proportionally), 2) Optimize code to use less memory, 3) Stream large files instead of loading entirely, 4) Implement pagination for large datasets. To increase: AWS Console > Lambda > Configuration > General > Memory, increase to 512MB or higher based on needs. Monitor MemorySize and MemoryUsed metrics. Best practices: use generators for large data processing, close database connections after use, clear large variables when done, avoid global variables holding large data. For very large processing (>10GB), consider using ECS Fargate or EC2 instead of Lambda. Enable detailed monitoring to track memory usage patterns.

prompt: Need help: Error: Server responded with HTTP 403 Forbidden
response: HTTP 403 means the server refused to authorize your request. prompt: API Gateway CORS error in browser
response: CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) error occurs when browser blocks request from different origin. Symptoms: works in Postman but fails in browser, see CORS error in browser console. Resolution: 1) Enable CORS in API Gateway for the API resource, 2) Add CORS headers to Lambda response, 3) Handle OPTIONS preflight requests. Required headers: Access-Control-Allow-Origin (set to specific domain or *), Access-Control-Allow-Headers (Content-Type, Authorization, etc.), Access-Control-Allow-Methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE). In Lambda: return {'statusCode': 200, 'headers': {'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*', 'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': 'Content-Type', 'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'GET,POST,OPTIONS'}, 'body': json.dumps(data)}. For OPTIONS requests, return 200 with CORS headers and empty body. Test from browser developer console.

Document which G/L accounts require profit center in interface specification.

prompt: Production issue: Payment terms Z030 is not defined in SAP
response: Payment terms error indicates configuration missing in SAP. Payment terms control invoice due dates and cash discounts. Resolution: 1) Check payment terms configuration using OBB8, 2) Create payment terms if missing, 3) Update interface mapping with valid payment terms, 4) Verify payment terms in vendor master matches. In OBB8: click 'New Entries', enter payment terms Z030, enter description (e.g., 'Net 30 days'), define payment terms details (baseline date, payment period), save. prompt: RDS database connection timeout
response: Connection timeout indicates network or security group issues. Troubleshooting steps: 1) Verify security group allows inbound traffic on port 5432 (PostgreSQL) or 3306 (MySQL) from application security group, 2) Check connection string format and credentials, 3) Test connectivity from application server using telnet or psql/mysql client, 4) Verify NAT Gateway exists if database is in private subnet, 5) Check route tables for proper routing. Common fix: Add inbound rule to RDS security group allowing Custom TCP on port 5432 from source security group of application servers. Also verify the endpoint URL is correct and database instance is running.

prompt: Production issue: ALB returns 502 Bad Gateway error
response: HTTP 502 from ALB means it received an invalid response from the target. Causes: 1) Target returned non-HTTP response, 2) Connection closed prematurely, 3) Target timeout exceeded, 4) Application returned malformed HTTP. Diagnostic steps: Check target health in target group, review ALB access logs in S3 for patterns, verify application logs for errors, ensure application returns valid HTTP response with proper status code and headers. Resolution: If timeout issue, increase target group timeout settings. If application error, fix application to return valid HTTP. If connection issue, check security groups allow ALB to reach targets on correct port. Enable ALB access logs for detailed troubleshooting.

prompt: SQS message visibility timeout causing duplicate processing
response: Duplicate processing occurs when visibility timeout is too short. When Lambda doesn't complete processing before timeout expires, message becomes visible again and is processed by another instance. Resolution: 1) Increase SQS visibility timeout to match or exceed Lambda timeout + buffer, 2) If Lambda takes 5 minutes, set visibility timeout to 6+ minutes, 3) Enable dead letter queue (DLQ) for messages that fail repeatedly, 4) Implement idempotency keys in application to handle duplicates gracefully. Configuration: AWS Console > SQS > Queue > Edit > Visibility timeout. Formula: Visibility timeout = Lambda timeout Ã— 6. Also implement unique message ID tracking in application to detect and skip duplicates. Monitor using CloudWatch metric ApproximateAgeOfOldestMessage.

prompt: In CTRM-SAP integration: API Gateway returning 429 Too Many Requests
response: HTTP 429 indicates rate limit exceeded. Default API Gateway limits: 10,000 requests/second steady state, 5,000 requests burst. Resolution: 1) Check current API usage in CloudWatch, 2) Request limit increase from AWS Support for account-level throttle, 3) Implement client-side retry with exponential backoff, 4) Use API keys and usage plans for better control, 5) Respect Retry-After header in response. For retry logic: wait_time = min(2^attempt, 60) seconds. To create usage plan: aws apigateway create-usage-plan --name BasicPlan --throttle burstLimit=100,rateLimit=50. Also consider caching frequently requested data to reduce backend calls and API invocations.

prompt: DLQ has messages but not processing
response: Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) collects messages that failed processing repeatedly. These require manual intervention. Resolution: 1) Retrieve messages from DLQ for analysis, 2) Identify failure pattern (data issue, code bug, infrastructure problem), 3) Fix root cause, 4) Reprocess valid messages, 5) Purge invalid messages. To retrieve: aws sqs receive-message --queue-url https://sqs.region.amazonaws.com/account/my-dlq. Common causes: poison pill messages with invalid data, code bugs that cause exception, timeout issues, external dependency failures. Process: fix the issue in code, redrive messages back to main queue: aws sqs start-message-move-task --source-arn <dlq-arn> --destination-arn <main-queue-arn>, or process manually with Lambda. Implement DLQ alarms: alert when NumberOfMessagesSent to DLQ > 0. Document DLQ handling procedures.
